key_insight: A latent space learned by a pretrained universal interatomic potential can act as a smooth evolutionary coordinate, enabling genetic search to preserve useful crystal motifs even when parent cells and symmetries differ.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical, decoder-free route to faster and more reliable crystal structure prediction and inverse materials design, especially for variable-supercell and superlattice problems.
method: Encode parent crystals with a pretrained potential to obtain latent vectors.
Interpolate parent latents to define a target offspring latent representation.
Generate offspring by inverse-optimizing atomic positions and lattice vectors to match the target latent.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved HfO2 ground-state recovery from 20-35% to 60-95%, nearly tenfold lower search cost over 16 perovskites, and discovery of new long-period superlattice ground states.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and transferability of the pretrained latent representation, so poor coverage of chemical space or inverse-optimization failures could limit robustness.
paper_title: Latent Genetic Algorithm for Crystal Structure Prediction
